description_short: PierPoint Mortgage LLC (NMLS #112844) is a multi-state mortgage lender offering conventional, government-backed, and alternative loan products across 19+ service areas including Seattle, Washington.
description_long: PierPoint Mortgage LLC is a licensed mortgage lender operating under NMLS #112844 with a presence in multiple states including Washington, Oregon, California, Colorado, Michigan, Connecticut, Virginia, Georgia, Florida, Louisiana, North Carolina, Oklahoma, Maine, and Alabama. The company serves the Seattle, Washington market as one of its designated service areas. PierPoint specializes in a broad range of mortgage products spanning traditional home purchase and refinancing options to more niche lending categories. Their offering includes conventional loans, FHA loans, VA loans, USDA loans, jumbo loans, and reverse mortgages for traditional borrowers. Beyond standard residential lending, PierPoint markets alternative products including DSCR/investor cash flow loans (1-8 units), bank statement loans, profit and loss-only loans, fix-and-flip loans, foreign national loans, manufactured home loans, hard money loans, new construction loans, and commercial loans. This comprehensive product suite positions them as a lender willing to work with non-traditional borrowers and investment property scenarios. PierPoint differentiates itself through the breadth of loan programs rather than specialization in a single niche. They offer free mortgage consultations, quick quote tools, and document upload capabilities on their website. The company maintains active online resources including a mortgage calculator, loan process explanations, FAQ section, credit education content, and customer testimonials. A notable limitation is that the Seattle service area page returns a 404 error, suggesting incomplete local market presence documentation or outdated website maintenance. While the company operates across multiple states and offers extensive product variety, the broken local landing page raises questions about the depth of Seattle-specific support and infrastructure. Prospective borrowers should verify current service availability and local loan officer assignment before applying.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Extensive product range including conventional, FHA, VA, USDA, jumbo, reverse mortgages, and investor loans
Serves 19+ states with dedicated service areas across major metropolitan regions
Offers non-traditional lending products (DSCR, bank statement, fix-and-flip, foreign national loans)
Free mortgage consultations and quick quote tools available on website
Licensed and regulated (NMLS #112844) with transparent contact information provided
Digital-first tools including secure document upload, mortgage calculator, and online application process
Educational resources including loan process guides, mortgage basics, and FAQ content
cons: Seattle, Washington service area page returns 404 error, indicating website maintenance issues or incomplete local presence documentation
No specific information about rates, fees, or loan terms available on accessible portions of website
Limited ability to verify current operational status and local staffing for Seattle market due to broken landing page
No clear information about minimum credit score requirements, loan amount limits, or eligibility criteria
Minimal customer testimonial details visible; unclear if feedback is specific to Seattle market or broader company experience
best_for: Investment property investors seeking DSCR loans and fix-and-flip financing across multiple states
Self-employed or non-traditional income borrowers who qualify for bank statement or profit/loss-only loans
Borrowers seeking conventional, FHA, VA, or USDA loans across PierPoint's 19+ service areas
diagnosis: PierPoint Mortgage LLC is best suited for borrowers seeking a multi-state lender with diverse loan products, particularly those with non-traditional income or investment property goals. However, prospective Seattle-area applicants should verify current local service availability due to a non-functional Seattle service area landing page, and should request specific rate and fee information before committing to application.
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
